behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: This failure is at the boundary between manifest validation and plugin execution, so wiring loader + manifest parser + plugin runner is required. Pass 2 downgrade: Unit is insufficient because it would only test validation logic and miss accidental module import side effects. Pass 2 upgrade: No behavior text requires subprocesses, real network, or AI output, so System/Agentic/Workflow are not required.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin directory with one plugin whose `manifest.json` omits a required field and whose entry module mutates a shared sentinel when imported. Run the real loader over that directory and assert: (1) loader reports manifest rejection, (2) sentinel remains unchanged (entry module never executed), (3) `onLoad` was not called.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's `onLoad` hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Requires dependency resolution and lifecycle execution working together across multiple plugins. Pass 2 downgrade: Unit test of topological sort alone would not catch executor-order bugs in actual hook invocation. Pass 2 upgrade: Behavior does not require subprocess/HTTP/OS primitives or AI output.
behaviors.test_description: Set up two real plugin packages in a temp directory: A depends on B. Each `onLoad` appends its name to a shared in-memory log. Run loader and assert observed call order is `[B, A]`.

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Needs resolver + load gating interaction (detect cycle and prevent both loads). Pass 2 downgrade: Unit cycle-detection test alone misses whether loader still invokes hooks despite detection. Pass 2 upgrade: No stated need for subprocesses, network, or model output.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B with mutual dependencies and `onLoad` hooks that write to a call log. Run loader and assert: (1) cycle error/report includes A<->B, (2) neither `onLoad` ran, (3) neither plugin is marked initialized.

behaviors.description: A plugin's `onLoad` hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: This is an ordering contract across module import execution and lifecycle dispatch. Pass 2 downgrade: Unit test of one function cannot validate real import side effects versus hook timing. Pass 2 upgrade: Behavior text does not require external process/network/OS-event semantics, only local module loading.
behaviors.test_description: Create a plugin whose entry module sets `globalThis.__entryExecuted = true` as a side effect and whose `onLoad` asserts that flag is already true; also record timestamps/order markers. Run loader and assert no assertion failure and order markers show `entry-executed` before `onLoad`.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its `onLoad` hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Requires interaction of error handling, dependency graph filtering, and continued initialization across plugins. Pass 2 downgrade: Unit-level try/catch logic cannot prove graph-aware continuation behavior. Pass 2 upgrade: No explicit subprocess/network/AI dependency in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins: F (throws in `onLoad`), D (depends on F), and C (independent). Run loader and assert: (1) F failure is reported, (2) D is skipped/not initialized, (3) C still initializes successfully.
